citations: SAD.md:§4 Technology Choices (lines 466-483) — technology stack aligns with ADR-1 through ADR-4
SAD.md:§1.3 Interceptor Chain Sequence (lines 97-111) — directly informs ADR-7 ordering
SAD.md:§2.5-2.7 adapters (lines 184-234) — adapter pattern validates ADR-6
SAD.md:§5.1 End-to-End Request Flow (lines 489-572) — confirms interceptor chain order in ADR-7
SRS.md:§2 Functional Requirements — all 22 FRs traceable to ADR decisions in the traceability matrix
SRS.md:§3 Non-Functional Requirements — all 10 NFRs mapped to ADR decisions
summary: 7 ADRs generated covering all major architecture decisions: FastAPI web framework (ADR-1), PostgreSQL 16 persistent storage (ADR-2), Redis 7 rate limiting (ADR-3), Docker Compose deployment (ADR-4), Pipeline Orchestrator pattern (ADR-5), Platform Adapter pattern (ADR-6), and Interceptor Chain Order (ADR-7). Each ADR includes Status, Context, Decision, Rationale with alternatives considered, and Consequences. A traceability matrix maps each ADR to SAD sections, FRs, and NFRs.
